steps.title: Close the interrupting phrase
steps.content: The phrase according to legal researchers James Melton and Tom Ginsburg interrupts the main clause between the subject Iceland's constitution and the verb contains. Since the phrase begins after a comma, it needs a comma at the end too.

steps.title: Keep the names attached
steps.content: Choice A leaves the interrupting phrase unclosed. Choice B splits legal researchers from the names and still lacks a closing comma. Choice D ends the sentence, leaving a fragment. A comma after Ginsburg closes the inserted phrase. The answer is C.
